hero.headline: SSODL Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Symbiosis International University's fully-online MBA with a Finance specialisation. NIRF #24 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Rs 55,000 work-experience scholarship for 2+ year professionals. Derivative Markets, Business Valuation, Investment Banking, and Financial Engineering in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: SSODL Online MBA Finance: Symbiosis brand (NIRF #24, NAAC A++), Rs 55K work-experience scholarship, capital markets depth with Derivative Markets, Mergers and Acquisitions, Investment Banking and Financial Engineering. Fees Rs 3.15L with scholarship, Rs 3.70L standard. Strongest for BFSI and investment banking careers in western India.

about.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Finance is offered by Symbiosis School for Online and Digital Learning, the fully-online arm of Symbiosis International University (SIU), Pune. SSODL received UGC-DEB approval in 2020-21 and is the online MBA arm of SIU, distinct from SCDL which offers distance diploma programmes.

SIU carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #24 University ranking (2025). The Finance specialisation covers capital markets at depth: Derivative Markets, Mergers and Acquisitions, Corporate Valuation, Investment Banking, and Financial Engineering in Sem 3-4. The tech-forward Sem 1-2 core (AI and ML for Business Management, R Programming, Data Driven Decision Making) is embedded across all SSODL MBA specs.

The programme suits working professionals in BFSI, corporate finance, and financial advisory who want the Symbiosis name for career advancement. The Rs 55,000 work-experience scholarship reduces the total to Rs 3.15L for applicants with 2+ years of experience.

sem1Subjects.name: Business Statistics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply probability distributions, regression, and statistical inference to financial data analysis and investment research.

sem1Subjects.name: Marketing Management
sem1Subjects.description: Understand customer value propositions and go-to-market strategy relevant to financial product distribution.

sem1Subjects.name: Financial Accounting
sem1Subjects.description: Prepare and interpret financial statements, including balance sheets and income statements, for corporate finance analysis.

sem1Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem1Subjects.description: Apply process efficiency concepts to banking operations, treasury back-office, and financial services delivery.

sem1Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem1Subjects.description: Apply company law, SEBI regulations, and contract frameworks relevant to financial transactions and compliance.

sem1Subjects.name: Human Resource Management
sem1Subjects.description: Manage finance team structure, analyst recruitment, and performance management for BFSI HR roles.

sem1Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem1Subjects.description: Design financial research studies, quantitative analysis frameworks, and data sourcing approaches for investment research.

sem1Subjects.name: Microeconomics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply price theory, market structure analysis, and demand-supply models to corporate pricing and competitive strategy.

sem1Subjects.name: Technology in Business
sem1Subjects.description: Evaluate fintech platforms, algorithmic trading concepts, and digital banking technology for finance professionals.

sem1Subjects.name: Data Driven Decision Making
sem1Subjects.description: Apply data analytics to credit risk assessment, portfolio performance tracking, and financial reporting automation.

sem2Subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
sem2Subjects.description: Manage finance and investment teams, build client relationships, and navigate deal team dynamics in BFSI.

sem2Subjects.name: Operations Research
sem2Subjects.description: Apply linear programming and optimisation models to portfolio construction and resource allocation.

sem2Subjects.name: Macroeconomics
sem2Subjects.description: Interpret GDP trends, interest rate cycles, and monetary policy impact on equity and bond markets.

sem2Subjects.name: Financial Management
sem2Subjects.description: Apply capital structure theory, WACC, DCF valuation, and working capital management to corporate finance decisions.

sem2Subjects.name: R Programming
sem2Subjects.description: Build financial models, run portfolio analysis, and automate return calculation using R.

sem2Subjects.name: Project Management
sem2Subjects.description: Apply project finance frameworks and timeline management to infrastructure and deal execution.

sem2Subjects.name: Management Information Systems
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate banking MIS, treasury management systems, and financial reporting platforms.

sem2Subjects.name: Business Communication
sem2Subjects.description: Write investment memos, credit notes, valuation reports, and board-level financial presentations.

sem2Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem2Subjects.description: Understand financial product consumer behaviour for retail banking, wealth management, and insurance marketing.

sem2Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem2Subjects.description: Apply digital acquisition strategy for BFSI product cross-sell, fintech customer onboarding, and investment platform growth.

sem2Subjects.name: Design Thinking
sem2Subjects.description: Apply human-centred design to financial product development, digital banking UX, and payment solution ideation.

sem2Subjects.name: Innovation Management
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate fintech innovation trends, open banking frameworks, and digital disruption in financial services.

sem3Subjects.name: Project I (4 Credits)
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct a structured financial research project on a company valuation, sector analysis, or investment thesis.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Governance and Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Apply board governance standards, ESG reporting frameworks, and financial ethics to corporate finance roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Crisis Management in Business
sem3Subjects.description: Manage financial distress scenarios, liquidity crisis communication, and restructuring decision frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Develop corporate strategy, M&A rationale, and competitive positioning for financial services firms.

sem3Subjects.name: AI and ML for Business Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply algorithmic credit scoring, fraud detection models, and robo-advisory concepts to BFSI technology roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Derivative Markets
sem3Subjects.description: Price options, futures, and swaps; apply hedging strategies for corporate treasury and fund management.

sem3Subjects.name: Mergers and Acquisitions
sem3Subjects.description: Understand deal sourcing, due diligence, synergy analysis, and post-merger integration for investment banking roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Valuation
sem3Subjects.description: Apply DCF, comparable company analysis, and precedent transaction multiples to company valuation for PE and IB.

sem3Subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build equity research frameworks, screen securities, and construct risk-adjusted portfolios using Modern Portfolio Theory.

sem4Subjects.name: Project II (10 Credits)
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a full finance dissertation applying valuation, capital markets, or financial strategy theory to a real-world case.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate startup funding structures, venture capital term sheets, and founder equity management from a finance perspective.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Business Environment
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse cross-border capital flows, sovereign risk, and international financial market dynamics for global finance roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Conflict and Negotiation
sem4Subjects.description: Negotiate deal terms, manage client disputes, and structure financial agreements in M&A and lending contexts.

sem4Subjects.name: Doing Business in India
sem4Subjects.description: Navigate RBI regulations, SEBI guidelines, Companies Act provisions, and India-specific capital market structures.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Transformation and Organizational Turnaround
sem4Subjects.description: Lead financial restructuring, debt resolution, and capital reallocation for distressed company turnarounds.

sem4Subjects.name: Investment Banking
sem4Subjects.description: Understand ECM, DCM, and M&A transaction processes including pitch book structure and deal execution mechanics.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Engineering and Analytics
sem4Subjects.description: Build structured financial products, apply quantitative models, and use analytics for fixed income and derivatives pricing.

sem4Subjects.name: International Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Manage FX risk, cross-border treasury operations, and currency hedging strategy for MNC and international banking.

sem4Subjects.name: Behavioral Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Apply cognitive bias research, investor sentiment analysis, and market anomaly theory to investment decision-making.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all SSODL MBA specialisations. The Finance specialisation adds 9 subjects in Sem 3 and 10 subjects in Sem 4.

sections.whoHires:
Finance MBA graduates from SSODL Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** BFSI, Investment banking, Corporate finance, Asset management, Big Four financial advisory

**Entry-level roles:** Corporate Finance Analyst, Treasury Analyst, Investment Banking Analyst, FP&A Analyst, Equity Research Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Corporate Finance Manager, VP Treasury, Portfolio Manager, Head of FP&A, M&A Associate

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO, MD (Investment Banking), Fund Manager, Head of Corporate Finance

**Top hiring organisations:** Kotak Mahindra Bank, HDFC Bank Corporate Banking, ICICI Bank Investment Banking, Axis Capital, JM Financial, Deloitte Financial Advisory, EY Transaction Advisory, KPMG Deal Advisory, Motilal Oswal, ICICI Lombard, SBI Life Insurance
sections.skills:
The SSODL Finance specialisation builds skills that BFSI and capital markets employers test at MBA-level interviews:

**Technical skills:**
- Corporate Valuation: DCF modelling, comparable company analysis, EV/EBITDA multiples, and M&A acquisition price justification for investment banking and PE fund roles
- Derivative Markets: options pricing, futures and forwards hedging strategy, and swap application for corporate treasury and risk management desk roles
- Security Analysis and Portfolio Management: equity research frameworks, portfolio construction, and risk-adjusted return analysis for wealth and asset management
- Financial Engineering and Analytics: structured product design, quantitative pricing models, and analytics for fixed income and derivatives teams

**Applied skills:**
- Investment Banking Process: ECM and DCM transaction mechanics, pitch book structure, and M&A deal process for analyst and associate roles
- Behavioral Finance: cognitive bias application, investor sentiment analysis, and anomaly-based strategy for fund management and wealth advisory

**Soft skills:** Presenting financial models to investment committees, writing credit memos for lending decisions, defending valuation assumptions in M&A negotiations, and structuring complex deal presentations for institutional clients.
sections.comparisons:
Comparing SSODL Online MBA Finance against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance and Accounting Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Finance covers accounting depth and taxation alongside financial management. SSODL Finance focuses on capital markets: Derivative Markets, Mergers and Acquisitions, Corporate Valuation, Investment Banking, and Financial Engineering. For investment banking and capital markets careers, SSODL Finance is more targeted. Amity is more competitive for CFO-track and accounting-heavy roles and at a lower fee.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management:** NMIMS is NAAC A++ at Rs 1.96L-2.20L with stronger Mumbai BFSI brand recognition. NMIMS Financial Management covers similar capital markets subjects. SSODL Finance adds Behavioral Finance as a dedicated subject and carries Symbiosis brand recognition in Pune-region BFSI. For Mumbai investment banking hiring, NMIMS carries a stronger brand advantage. For Pune and western India BFSI, SSODL is competitive.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Finance:** MUJ is NAAC A+ and NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000-1,80,000 with Coursera Plus. MUJ Finance covers corporate finance broadly with a more affordable fee. SSODL carries stronger brand recognition in BFSI hiring in western India and the Pune corporate ecosystem.

fees.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Finance offers 2 fee tracks based on applicant work experience:

**Track 1 — Standard (under 2 years work experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,70,000 across 4 semesters
- Semester breakdown: Rs 92,500 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 87,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,95,000 (including Rs 10,000 registration)
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,75,000

**Track 2 — With Work Experience Scholarship (2+ years experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,15,000 after Rs 55,000 experience-based scholarship
- Semester breakdown: Rs 80,000 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 72,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,70,000
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,45,000

The Rs 55,000 experience scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or opt for one-time upfront payment. Submit 2+ years work experience proof during admission to claim.

EMI is available through the university's marketing partner. This is interest-bearing EMI, not zero-cost. Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options and approval terms.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with SSODL each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and scholarship eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Symbiosis International University (SIU) and carries the SIU name with the MBA designation. The Finance specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India. SSODL is separate from SCDL; both are Symbiosis entities, but SSODL is the UGC-DEB approved online MBA arm.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from SSODL Online MBA Finance alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Arjun Mehta
items.liked: Corporate Valuation and Derivative Markets were at the level of finance subjects I would expect from a premium programme. Investment Banking module covered deal mechanics that I immediately applied in my new role.
items.disliked: Some elective faculty were adjunct and less interactive. Could have benefited from more live case studies.
items.body: I joined from a 4-year treasury analyst role and targeted corporate finance leadership. The Symbiosis brand got me shortlisted for roles at Pune-headquartered BFSI companies where I had struggled before. The work-exp scholarship brought the fee to Rs 3.15L, which was fair for the brand value.

items.name: Kavitha Subramanian
items.liked: Behavioral Finance was genuinely insightful. R Programming in Sem 2 helped me build financial models that impressed interview panels.
items.disliked: The Rs 3.70L standard fee is high compared to alternatives. Interest-bearing EMI adds further cost — zero-cost EMI would have been much better.
items.body: Good programme with strong capital markets depth. The Symbiosis name helped in Chennai BFSI hiring more than I expected. However, if cost is a major factor, Amity or MUJ Finance at lower fees are worth comparing.

items.name: Rahul Srivastava
items.liked: Strong subject coverage, especially Mergers and Acquisitions and Security Analysis.
items.disliked: Placement support was limited to a job portal. Active placement outreach for investment banking roles did not happen. Had to self-source interviews.
items.body: The programme content is solid and the Symbiosis name matters. But for investment banking roles in Mumbai, the NMIMS brand carries stronger recognition at top investment banks. Useful programme — just set realistic placement expectations.

faqs.question: How does SSODL's work-experience scholarship work?
faqs.answer: Applicants with 2 or more years of documented work experience receive a Rs 55,000 scholarship off the standard Rs 3,70,000 program fee. The scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or in a single upfront payment. Submit proof of work experience during the admission process to have it applied automatically.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Online MBA the same as Symbiosis on-campus MBA?
faqs.answer: Both are awarded by Symbiosis International University and carry UGC recognition. The on-campus MBA programmes (SIBM, SCMHRD, etc.) are full-time residential. SSODL Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and fully online. The university name on the certificate is the same; the mode differs. Most corporate employers treat UGC-DEB approved online MBAs as equivalent for eligibility.

faqs.question: Does SSODL offer no-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: No. SSODL EMI is interest-bearing and routed through the university's marketing partner. This differs from MUJ and NMIMS which offer zero-cost EMI. Factor in EMI interest cost when comparing total programme cost.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Finance stronger for investment banking or corporate finance?
faqs.answer: SSODL Finance covers both tracks. Investment Banking and Mergers and Acquisitions in Sem 4 target IB analyst roles. Corporate Valuation and Security Analysis support both PE and corporate finance tracks. The programme is broad enough for either path, though Mumbai-based investment banking firms tend to prefer NMIMS or on-campus MBA brands.

faqs.question: Does the SSODL Finance MBA cover CFA-relevant content?
faqs.answer: Several subjects overlap with CFA Level 1 content: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management, Derivative Markets, Behavioral Finance, and International Finance. The SSODL MBA is not a CFA substitute, but the overlap can reduce preparation effort for professionals pursuing both simultaneously.

chooseIf: You want the Symbiosis International University brand for BFSI and capital markets careers in Pune and western India.
You have 2+ years work experience and the Rs 55,000 scholarship reduces the effective fee to Rs 3.15L.
You want capital markets depth: Derivative Markets, Corporate Valuation, Investment Banking, and Financial Engineering in a single programme.
consider: If Mumbai investment banking brand recognition is the priority, NMIMS Online MBA Financial Management carries stronger recognition at Mumbai-headquartered investment banks and asset managers.
If fee is the primary concern, Amity Online MBA Finance (Rs 2.25L) and MUJ Online MBA Finance (Rs 1.53L-1.80L) offer good alternatives at lower costs.
If zero-cost EMI matters, MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI; SSODL does not.
